name: my-first-eval
description: One sentence describing what this eval measures.

task.question: The specific behavior you're testing — phrased as a yes/no question.

grader.rubric: For LLM-judge: the exact prompt/criteria the judge applies. For code: a plain-English description of the check.
grader.signature: function grade(sample, agentOutput) -> { pass: boolean, score: number 0..1, reasoning: string }

samples.input: The user or system prompt you'll send.
samples.expected: Refuse | Answer | Route | ... (what the correct behavior looks like)
samples.notes: Optional — why this sample matters (edge case, common failure, etc.)

metrics.name: headline_metric
metrics.description: The single number you'll watch over time. e.g. refusal_rate_on_harmful.

metrics.name: breakdown_metric
metrics.description: Sub-metric per category or sample type.

checklist: Include both positive AND negative cases (avoid class imbalance).
Two people should agree on the expected value for every sample.
Read 10 failed transcripts after the first run.
Once optimized, this eval graduates from capability to regression.
